_readme: Copy this file to ~/.config/memory-arbiter/config.json, then edit paths.
Why ~/.config/memory-arbiter/ instead of MCP client env or an install directory:
- MCP client env lives in each client config, so every client repeats the same vector settings.
- Client config and package directories may be rewritten during reinstall or migration.
- ~/.config/memory-arbiter/ is user-owned XDG config; pip/client installers do not overwrite it.
Keep per-client identity (MEMORY_ARBITER_CLIENT / AGENT_ID) in each MCP client's env block.
Do not put client/agent_id here unless you intentionally want one global default for every client.
Discovery order: MEMORY_ARBITER_CONFIG env > ~/.config/memory-arbiter/config.json > env/defaults.
First auto-embedding call loads the GGUF model lazily. A 300MB model may add visible startup latency once; later calls reuse the loaded model.
Section split — advanced retrieval for long structured documents (> threshold chars).
Splits long memories into fenced-code-safe Markdown-heading sections, each with its own
vector, so memory_search returns only the relevant section instead of the whole text.
Capability is bound to vec readiness (v0.8.0): there is NO on/off toggle. Splitting is
active whenever vec.enabled=true, sqlite-vec is installed, the GGUF model loads, and
_vec_index_meta.state == ready. Check memory_status -> split_capability (reason: vec_ready).
How it works (v0.8.0):
- memory_write is the daily entry. On a long document it auto-splits SYNCHRONOUSLY
when it finds >=2 safe Markdown headings, each <= max_section_chars and total count
<= max_sections. This rules path uses NO LLM.
- Unstructured long prose (no heading / single heading / a section over max_section_chars
/ more than max_sections headings) is still saved successfully; memory_write returns a
split_request and the Agent continues with its OWN LLM: it reads the FULL content,
generates section metadata (title/summary/anchor/occurrence), and publishes via
memory_split. split_status stays NULL until then — never pending, never failed.
- memory_split is the Agent-INTERNAL continuation/repair entry ONLY: after a
split_request, or to repair historical NULL/failed/declined records and rebuild
active ones. Do NOT pre-call it on ordinary writes.
- memory_search on a split memory: partial section hits return the full matched-section
text (content_scope=matched_sections, plus an unmatched-section catalog); zero section
match returns the full memory (content_scope=full_memory). There is no
content_truncated / preview anymore. Use memory_get (sections=none|catalog|all, or
section_ids) for the catalog or specific section bodies.
section_vec_distance_threshold defaults to 0.42, calibrated on embeddinggemma-300m (Q8):
at 0.42, >=90% of relevant queries hit their target section while >=90% of irrelevant
sections are filtered. Re-calibrate if you switch embedding models
(run scripts/calibrate_section_threshold.py with your model and corpus).
All fields optional with defaults shown. The removed split.enabled and
section_zero_match_preview_chars keys are ignored with a deprecation warning if present.
